title: China factory activity grows faster than expected in June on tech export demand
description: China's manufacturing engine held up this year as surging demand for artificial intelligence technology helped offset the drag from Middle East turmoil.

title: Japanese yen sinks to 40-year low, keeping intervention risks in focus
description: The Japanese yen weakened to its lowest level against the U.S. dollar since 1986 on Tuesday, keeping investors on alert for possible intervention from Japanese authorities.

title: Australia's competition regulator takes Amazon to court over alleged unfair Prime subscription contract terms
description: Australia's competition regulator is taking Amazon to court, alleging its Prime contracts required subscribers to pay AU$2.99 to avoid advertising, with no option for refunds

title: Apple supplier Luxshare seeks up to $3.1 billion in Hong Kong share sale
description: Apple supplier Luxshare Precision Industry is seeking to raise as much as $24.27 billion Hong Kong dollars ($3.1 billion) through a Hong Kong share sale.

title: Japan is raising visa fees by up to 400% for the first time in nearly 50 years. Here's why
description: A single entry visa will climb to 15,000 yen, from the 3,000 yen charged currently, while multiple entry visas will cost 30,000 yen, up from the 6,000 yen now.

title: Magnificent Seven stocks shed $2.3tn in Wall Street tech rotation
description: Investors switch to soaring chipmakers benefiting from hyperscalers’ vast AI spending

title: Private equity fund investors turn to debt-like deals in downturn
description: Backers of buyout funds agreed $9bn worth of ‘alternative’ transactions last year, up from $6bn in 2024

title: Can Kazakhstan’s oil boom survive Putin’s War? | FT Film
description: Central Asia's largest economy depends on an export pipeline that crosses Russia

title: US Supreme Court blocks firing of Fed governor
description: In separate decision, justices said President Donald Trump can sack top regulator

title: Yen weakens to 40-year low
description: Japanese currency slides past ¥162 as Federal Reserve’s hawkish shift piles on pressure

title: Trump Lost the Fed Battle, But Look What He Won
description: The Supreme Court cleared his right to fire regulators at will — like in markets, crypto.

title: Morgan Stanley Warns of Oil Glut and Cuts Forecasts on Hormuz
description: Morgan Stanley cut oil forecasts for the second time in about two weeks as flows through the Strait of Hormuz return faster than expected, while strong US supply and weak Chinese demand raise the risk of a glut.

title: Breitling Owner: China Remains a Complicated Market
description: Georges Kern, CEO of House of Brands, says that although the group is gaining market share in China, consumers remain reluctant to invest in luxury goods. He also shares more about the group's expansion plans on "Bloomberg: The China Show." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Miniso US CEO on Business Growth Strategy
description: Tom Bartlebaugh, CEO of Miniso USA, discusses his outlook for the Chinese brand's expansion into North America, and its strategy in expanding on lifestyle and apparel products, utilising collaborations and original IP. He says the company targets 20% in US store growth this year. He speaks exclusively with Haidi Stroud-Watts on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Japan’s Two-Year Bond Sale Demand Stronger Than 12-Month Average
description: Japan’s two-year government bond auction Tuesday saw stronger demand than the 12-month average as elevated yields underpinned buying.

title: China PMI Lays Relatively Constructive Picture for June: BofA
description: China’s factory and non-manufacturing activity fared better than expected in June. BofA's Helen Qiao says the supply-side factors have been "pretty resilient for some time," but the "question is really on the demand-side - consumption and investment." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Indonesia Capital Flight Caps Most-Profitable Bank’s Wealth Push
description: Indonesia’s biggest non-state bank said capital flight has hampered efforts to expand its wealth management business, underscoring how an erosion of investor confidence is hindering the private sector in Southeast Asia’s largest economy.

title: Australians' Trust in US Hits Record Low
description: A new Lowy Institute survey points to a significant shift in Australians' views on China, the US alliance, and President Trump. Research Fellow Charles Lyons-Jones joins "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" to unpack the results and their implications for Australia's strategic positioning in the Indo-Pacific. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Broker Stocks Eye Best Month Since October on Trading Boom
description: Chinese brokerage stocks are set to close June with their strongest monthly gain in eight months, driven by rising trading activity and IPO issuance that may sustain the rally.

title: Indonesia Biofuel Push Adds Strain to Producers and Palm Markets
description: The rollout of a pioneering palm-diesel blend is set to stretch Indonesian biofuel makers to their limits and tighten global supplies of the tropical oil by diverting it away from export markets.

title: Record Bond Inflows Ease India Equity Exodus
description: Global funds invested $5.2 billion in index-eligible bonds this month.

title: Indonesian Bonds Draw $1.2 Billion as Higher Yields Lure Funds
description: Indonesian bonds are on track for their biggest monthly inflow in more than a year, in a sign that government efforts to lift yields to lure foreign investors and support the rupiah are paying off.

title: Yen Hits Four-Decade Low in Historic Slide
description: The yen slid to its weakest level against the dollar since 1986, a milestone that will generate unease in Japan and put traders on high alert for authorities wading into the market. Bloomberg's Ruth Carson explains the context. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Pakistan Buys Emergency LNG Shipment on Disrupted Qatar Exports
description: Pakistan purchased a liquefied natural gas shipment for prompt delivery this week as tension in the Strait of Hormuz continues to disrupt Qatari exports to the South Asian nation.

title: Smuggler Pleads Guilty in $30 Million Scheme to Move Immigrants
description: A criminal network generated up to $30 million from smuggling immigrants across the southern border into the US, according to a statement from the Justice Department.

title: China Clamps Down on Issuance of Higher-Yielding Offshore Debt
description: Chinese authorities are discouraging firms from raising money offshore at higher yields, in the latest step to clamp down on risks among municipal borrowers shouldering more than $100 billion of overseas bond debt, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Mizuho, SMFG Fuel Record Japanese Corporate Bond Sales Overseas
description: Mizuho Financial Group Inc. and Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group Inc. propelled foreign-currency bond sales by Japanese companies to a record for any quarter Monday as the nation’s companies tap deeper overseas markets to finance growth.

title: How Ambani Built Project Jupiter to Launch India’s Biggest IPO
description: Last August, Mukesh Ambani promised shareholders that Jio Platforms Ltd. would seek a stock market listing in the first half of 2026.

title: AirPods Maker Luxshare Seeks Up to $3.1 Billion From Hong Kong Listing
description: Apple Inc. supplier Luxshare Precision Industry Co. has started taking investor orders for its Hong Kong listing that may fetch as much as HK$24.3 billion ($3.1 billion), the city’s biggest share debut so far this year.

title: What do we know about the shooting in Stade, Germany?
description: The BBC's Jessica Parker reports from the scene of the shooting that killed six people in northern Germany.

title: Chinese tycoon sentenced to 30 years in US jail
description: Guo Wengui branded himself as a China critic, gaining followers who backed his fraudulent schemes.

title: India's biggest share sales tell the story of a country glued to its phones
description: NSE and Jio Platforms embody the sweeping changes in how India has consumed, invested and transacted over the past decade.

title: Norway’s ‘Viking Row’ Is a Fan Phenomenon at the World Cup
description: Seas of red-clad Norwegian supporters are rowing shoulder to shoulder at stadiums, schools, even a nursing home. Not everyone is thrilled.

title: Former Chinese Billionaire and Bannon Associate Is Sentenced for Fraud
description: The businessman courted U.S. conservatives as an anti-Communist crusader while using his supporters’ money to buy lavish homes and a Bugatti supercar.

title: How Germany were handed a World Cup humbling and what it means to Paraguay
description: As well as Gill's heroics, Paraguay's unlikely victory was founded on coach Alfaro's unshakeable faith in his defenders.

title: US education faces ‘a crisis for low-wealth students’
description: Andre Perry tells This is America that going to college still pays, but low-wealth students carry most of the debt.

title: Police hunt for suspect after three wounded in Monaco blast
description: Ukrainian oligarch reported to be among injured in explosion at residential building in the Mediterranean principality.

title: Mourners light candles after deadly German shelter shooting
description: Residents lit candles on Monday evening near the site of a shooting that killed six staff members at a German shelter.

title: Trump: US envoys enroute to Doha for ‘perhaps important’ Iran meeting
description: President Donald Trump said US negotiators are heading to Qatar for what he called a ‘perhaps important’ meeting.

title: UN ordering 10,000 body bags for Venezuela as death toll expected to rise
description: The UN says it is procuring 10,000 body bags for Venezuela, warning the death toll will rise.

title: Fears for people deported from US to Venezuela hours before earthquakes hit
description: Search for deportees from US continues in the rubble of the hotel they were taken to in Venezuela's La Guaira city.

title: Fear grows in Johannesburg market as anti-migrant ‘deadline’ looms
description: An unofficial June 30 deadline was set by anti-immigration groups for undocumented migrants to leave South Africa.

title: Paraguay coach salutes ‘extraordinary’ World Cup win over Germany
description: Paraguay produced arguably the greatest World Cup upset as Germany was defeated 4-3 on penalties in the last 32.

title: Families hold out hope for survivors five days after Venezuela earthquakes
description: Search and rescue operations continue in Caracas, Venezuela nearly five days after the devastating double earthquakes.

title: Keiko Fujimori leads in Peruvian presidential race as vote count concludes
description: Daughter of former President Alberto Fujimori says the country is closer to 'order and hope' after prolonged vote count.

title: Migrants in South Africa fear violence ahead of June 30 deadline
description: An anti-immigrant June 30 deadline not recognised by authorities is fuelling fear among migrants.

title: Iran war live: Tehran denies Trump’s claim of meeting in Doha
description: Trump says meeting with Iran will take place in Doha today, but Tehran says there are no plans for talks in coming days.

title: Funding option: royalties giant opens Hong Kong base as mainland China biotech deals surge
description: The world’s largest buyer of biopharmaceutical royalties has set up its first Asia-Pacific base in Hong Kong, joining a string of multinational corporations in the pharmaceutical sector establishing offices in the city as out-licensing deals by mainland Chinese biotechnology firms hit record highs. Royalty financing gives drug developers an alternative way to raise capital even as US investment restrictions loom, according to an expert. “Chinese biotech firms would need royalty financing as an...

title: Hong Kong IPOs ride China’s tech wave, but Nasdaq leads way with SpaceX tsunami
description: Hong Kong has surrendered its IPO fundraising crown to Nasdaq following SpaceX’s blockbuster listing earlier this month, but it did so while seeing its IPO proceeds register a year-on-year rise of about 84 per cent in the first half of 2026 – consolidating the region’s position as a primary offshore financial hub for Chinese issuers. A total of 83 companies raised US$26.42 billion on the Hong Kong stock exchange’s main board from January to June, according to LSEG Data & Analytics. The figure,...

title: Shanghai should step up as finance hub, as Hong Kong ‘not enough’, proposal says
description: Shanghai has been urged to build itself into a hub serving the rising outbound investment needs of Chinese firms, potentially increasing rivalry with Hong Kong as both cities race to augment their status as financial centres. The suggestion by Liu Xiaochun, vice-president of the Shanghai Finance Institute and a senior banker with three decades of experience, was made in mid-June at a closed-door meeting hosted by China Finance 40, a Beijing think tank comprising many top Chinese financial...

title: Not Even Musk Has The Answer To AI’s Power Shortage
description: The White House issued a stark warning in July 2025: Without $1.4 trillion in new infrastructure investment, electricity prices could surge as much as 58% by 2030. That’s driven mostly by the insatiable power demand from the rise of AI data centers and cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin. According to Axios, new reports show that power demand is set to grow up to 10x between now and 2030. The grid we’ve relied on for decades simply can't keep pace, and that’s threatening to put a major strain on both the AI and Bitcoin booms that have…

title: The AI jobs debate just got messier
description: A new report finds "high-intensity AI adopters” saw headcount increase 10.2%. Among those companies, entry-level headcount rose by 12%, countering the rhetoric that AI kills junior jobs.

title: Vibe coding platform Base44 launches own model as AI startups seek defensibility
description: Wix-owned vibe coding platform Base44 has started rolling out its own AI model — with hopes that it will eventually outperform frontier models.

title: China factory activity grows faster than expected in June on tech export demand
description: China's manufacturing engine held up this year as surging demand for artificial intelligence technology helped offset the drag from Middle East turmoil.

description: The Japanese yen weakened to its lowest level against the U.S. dollar since 1986 on Tuesday, keeping investors on alert for possible intervention from Japanese authorities.

description: Apple supplier Luxshare Precision Industry is seeking to raise as much as $24.27 billion Hong Kong dollars ($3.1 billion) through a Hong Kong share sale.

title: AeroVironment soars 19% on earnings beat, backlog grows to $1.2 billion
description: Dronemaker AeroVironment's autonomous system revenue beat expectations by $90 million and its backlog continued to grow.

title: Japan is raising visa fees by up to 400% for the first time in nearly 50 years. Here's why
description: A single entry visa will climb to 15,000 yen, from the 3,000 yen charged currently, while multiple entry visas will cost 30,000 yen, up from the 6,000 yen now.

title: Waymo and Uber end robotaxi pilot in Phoenix
description: The Waymo self-driving cars deployed in Phoenix for the Uber pilot will remain in use and make autonomous deliveries with DoorDash.

title: Trump’s weekend Iran strikes keep sparking Monday stock rallies. Here’s what the data shows us.
description: Call it the ‘Axios put’: On average, stocks have been up more on Mondays in the second quarter than in recent years

description: Gold extended a decline below $4,000 an ounce as the US and Iran sent conflicting signals ahead of fresh talks to end the war that’s fueled global inflationary pressures.
